steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: Left side: an independent clause (“The Mission 66 initiative... represented a major investment in the infrastructure of overburdened national parks”). Right side: an independent clause (“it prioritized physical improvements...”).

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content: A semicolon properly separates two independent clauses. Option A pairs a conjunction with a noun phrase instead of two clauses, Option B omits punctuation entirely (creating a run-on), and Option D uses only a comma (creating a comma splice). That matches choice C — C is correct.
